$schema: Hierarchical benchmark thresholds — mirrors the nbomber-*-thresholds.json pattern. See ADR-025 and performance-measurement-methodology.md.
description: Performance thresholds for BenchmarkDotNet microbenchmarks. Values marked TBD will be calibrated from the first weekly 'short' run with real N>10 data.

global.description: Global defaults. maxCov=10% is the stability threshold from the methodology doc. Benchmarks exceeding it are flagged Unstable.

mediator.description: Core mediator pipeline — hot path, single-digit microseconds expected

Send_Command_WithInstrumentation.description: Single handler dispatch with DI resolution + OTel span

delegates.description: Delegate invocation strategies — nanosecond-level overhead comparison

CompiledDelegate.description: Compiled expression tree delegate — must be within 2x of DirectCall

ExpressionCompilation.description: One-time compilation cost — acceptable up to 100us

cache.description: Handler/query cache lookup — nanosecond-level for TryGetValue

Send_Command_CacheHit.description: Full Send with cached handler resolution

validation.description: Validation provider comparison — equivalent latency expected across providers

FluentValidation_ValidCommand.description: FluentValidation — should be within 20% of other providers

LeastConnections_SelectReplica.description: Lock + ConcurrentDictionary — higher but still sub-microsecond

polly.description: Resilience policy overhead — should be negligible vs no-policy baseline

WithRetryAttribute_NoActualRetries.description: Must be within 20% of baseline (retry path not triggered)

audit-marten.description: Temporal encryption for audit events — AES-256-GCM performance

Encrypt_Short_16B.description: Short payload encryption — baseline for overhead measurement

GetExistingKey.description: In-memory key lookup — must be sub-microsecond

EncryptAuditEntry_Full_AllPii.description: Full entry with 6 PII fields — encryption less than 1% of DB IO cost

security-encryption.description: AES-256-GCM field encryption — sub-microsecond for short strings

security-antitampering.description: HMAC signing/verification — sub-microsecond for small payloads

security-sanitization.description: Output encoding — nanosecond-level for safe text, sub-microsecond for special chars

security-pii.description: PII masking — sub-microsecond per field, about 2us for multi-field objects

inbox-efcore.description: EF Core inbox store — higher latency due to DbContext overhead

AddMessage.description: Single message add via EF Core — includes change tracking

_notes.TBD_calibration: Values are initial estimates based on dry-mode data and existing documentation. After the first weekly short run produces N>10 data, run: dotnet run .github/scripts/check-benchmarks.cs to validate these thresholds and adjust where needed.
_notes.threshold_policy: Thresholds represent the MAXIMUM acceptable value. A benchmark exceeding its threshold is a regression. Thresholds should be about 2x the typical measured value to allow for cloud-runner variance.
_notes.methodology_reference: See docs/testing/performance-measurement-methodology.md for CoV, CI99, and regression detection formulas.
